_comment: Manual Mission Packets — the hand-authored feed source. Each entry follows the Mission Packet model (see ../PACKET_MODEL.md). REQUIRED: id, title, sourceType. Recommended: source, timestamp, severity, status, summary. severity: critical | high | medium | low | info. status: open | in-progress | blocked | resolved | watching | unknown. rawStatus: live | partial | placeholder | not wired (default 'manual' if omitted — but use the model's words honestly). ownerAction true = surfaces in 'Owner Action Now'. confidence: high | medium | low. timestamp: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) or the honest word 'reported'/'unknown' — the page computes freshness from real dates and shows 'unknown' otherwise. NEVER label a packet 'live'/'green'/'merged' unless it is actually true and verified.

items.title: Mission Control Feed layer shipped (this lane)
items.summary: New /lab/mission-control/feed page adds the Mission Packet model + adapter layer, an honest Wiring Map, and the Agent Feed Inbox scaffold. Renders from feed/data/*.json and normalizes the existing v1 registry. No backend, no secrets, nothing faked live.

related.build: no CI for /lab; coverage gate unaffected

items.ownerActionText: Browser-check /lab/mission-control/feed/ on the branch preview, then approve/merge (owner-only).

items.title: PR feed not wired yet — cards are reported, not live
items.summary: The PR / Branch feed shows packets normalized from the v1 reported PR board. These are NOT live from GitHub. A safe live feed requires a server-side Worker holding the GitHub token — never a client-side token.
evidence: No GitHub API call is made from the browser
Upgrade path documented in feed/data/feeds.json → pr-feed.needsNext
items.ownerActionText: Decide whether to greenlight a server-side GitHub feed Worker (owner-only infra lane; needs explicit OWNER-OK).

items.title: Cloudflare Access for /lab/* — verify before sharing any preview
items.summary: The only real gate on /lab/* is Cloudflare Access, configured in the Cloudflare dashboard (not in this repo). noindex + robots.txt are crawler hygiene, NOT access control. If the page loads with no Access prompt, it is not gated.
evidence: Open /lab/mission-control/feed/ in an incognito window — expect a Cloudflare Access sign-in first
items.ownerActionText: Confirm Cloudflare Zero Trust Access covers /lab and /lab/* with Allow = your email(s) before sharing any deployed link.

items.title: Security / dependency feed — placeholder
items.summary: No Dependabot / security-advisory feed is wired into Mission Control yet. This placeholder exists so the gap is visible rather than silently missing.

items.title: growverse-api health — reported down (login 404)
items.summary: Reported only — Mission Control does not poll any /health endpoint yet. growverse.dev login 404s because no healthy backend is wired. This packet mirrors the v1 owner-action queue for visibility; it is not a live health check.
evidence: Not verifiable from this session — treat as reported
items.ownerActionText: From your authenticated machine: bring growverse-api up, confirm /health = 200, then wire Vercel BACKEND_URL. Not runnable from Mission Control.

items.title: Coverage gate is the only real CI — not yet surfaced live here
items.summary: The repo has one real GitHub Actions gate — coverage on leaderboard/shared/ (.github/workflows/coverage.yml). It runs on push/PR but is NOT read into Mission Control. This lane touches only lab/ + docs/, so that gate is unaffected. No build/CI status shown here is live.
evidence: This /lab + docs lane does not change leaderboard/shared/ — coverage gate unaffected
Surfacing real CI requires the server-side Worker — see feed/data/feeds.json → ci-feed

items.title: No release feed configured
items.summary: There is no tagged-release feed yet. Listed so the absence is explicit, not assumed.
